---
title: "Supervised Theses"
subtitle: "Bachelor's and Master's Thesis Projects"
description-meta: "List of supervised thesis projects by Peter Kapec"
page-navigation: false
title-block-banner: false
---

## Overview

Over the years, I have supervised more than **40 bachelor's and master's thesis projects** across various topics in computer science, with a focus on visualization, graphics, and software engineering.

## Interested?

Search in <strong>Yonban</strong> for my other projects - not all were assigned to students. <br>
If the projects I am/was supervising are interesting and you would like to work on similar project, feel free to contact me.
<br>
However the process of defining new projects/thesis is a bit complicated, so for:
<ul>
<li><strong>Bachelor project</strong> - contact me via mail in your 2nd undergraduate study year (mid of summer semester)</li>
<li><strong>Master thesis</strong> - TO DO!</li>
</ul>

### Research Areas

The supervised theses cover a wide range of topics including:

- **Software Visualization** - 3D visualization of software architecture and dependencies
- **Graph Algorithms** - Layout algorithms, clustering, and edge bundling techniques
- **Augmented Reality** - AR applications for data and software visualization
- **Interactive Visualization** - Web-based and desktop visualization tools
- **Data Mining** - Visual data mining and analytics
- **Multimedia** - Video processing, 3D graphics, and multimedia systems
- **Web Technologies** - HTML5, WebGL, and interactive web applications

---

## Current Opportunities

I am always looking for motivated students interested in:
- Software visualization and visual analytics
- 3D graphics and augmented reality
- Graph algorithms and network visualization
- Interactive data visualization
- Web-based visualization tools

If you're interested in working on a thesis project in these areas, please [contact me](contact.qmd) to discuss potential topics.

### What I Look For

- Strong programming skills (Java, JavaScript, Python, C++)
- Interest in visualization and graphics
- Motivation to explore new technologies
- Ability to work independently
- Good communication skills

---

## Thesis Projects

Browse all supervised thesis projects — searchable and filterable by type, topic, and year.

```{=html}
<a href="supervised_theses_v2/index.html" style="
  display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: .5rem;
  margin-top: .5rem;
  padding: .6rem 1.4rem; border-radius: 7px;
  background: #111; color: #fff; font-size: .925rem; font-weight: 600;
  text-decoration: none; transition: background .18s;
" onmouseover="this.style.background='#333'" onmouseout="this.style.background='#111'">
  <svg width="15" height="15" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><rect x="3" y="3" width="7" height="7"/><rect x="14" y="3" width="7" height="7"/><rect x="3" y="14" width="7" height="7"/><rect x="14" y="14" width="7" height="7"/></svg>
  Browse Thesis Projects
</a>
```
